title: auth/DoA/doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:113 › Delegated Organisation - Notification Creation › Carol Clark (agent) creates for Plant Org 1 → contact and importer auto-populated from Plant Org 1
newSurface: This delegated-agent (DoA) CHED-PP creation exercises the entire agent surface the standard journey never renders. (1) A TWO-STEP organisation selector inserted right after the CHED-PP consignment-type page: first 'consignment-for' (Who are you creating this notification for?) offering the agent's OWN organisation(s) as radios (e.g. 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C') plus a final radio 'A different organisation'; choosing that leads to 'consignment-organisation' (Which company is this notification for) which lists every trade-partner the agent is authorised to act for as radios, each rendered with organisation name in bold + full multi-line address (IPAFFS Plant Agency F — 22 Acacia Avenue, London, N17 8LR; IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1 — KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT; IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2 — same address). (2) A persistent 'Trade Partner' badge: on the dashboard each notification created for a delegated org shows, under the 'CHED status' term, a 'Draft CHEDPP' strong tag followed by a 'Trade Partner' strong badge, and the Consignee column resolves to the delegated org ('IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1'). (3) Contact/importer AUTO-POPULATION from the delegated org, NOT the signed-in agent: the Responsible person / Contact details resolve to a MEMBER of Plant Org 1 (Isabel Irwin, ipaffs-plant-org-1-isabel@mailinator.com, tel 0123456789) and the Importer resolves to the delegated org itself (IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1, KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT, GB, 0123456789, test@example.com) — the agent (Carol Clark) never types responsible-person or importer details; the journey skips straight from consignor/exporter to declaration. (4) Agent header nav: the signed-in banner reads 'Carol Clark |' and carries extra links 'Address book', 'Manage account', 'Manage trade partners' (/trade-partners/filter), 'Sign out' — the trade-partner management entry points.

dashboard-trade-partner-badge.location: dashboard row 'CHED status' definition, and Consignee column
dashboard-trade-partner-badge.note: 'Trade Partner' rendered as a <strong> badge beneath the CHED status on every notification created on behalf of a delegated org.

authorizationRules: Carol Clark is a delegated agent authorised to create CHED-PP notifications on behalf of multiple trade-partner organisations: IPAFFS Plant Agency F, IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1, and IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2 — all offered as selectable radios on the consignment-organisation picker (confirmed, trace action 13).
The agent may also create for their own organisation(s) (e.g. IPAFFS Plant Agency C) — offered on the consignment-for page alongside the 'A different organisation' route (confirmed, trace action 11).

When creating for a delegated org, the Responsible person / Contact details are auto-populated from a MEMBER of that delegated org (Isabel Irwin of Plant Org 1) — NOT from the signed-in agent (Carol Clark). The agent's own identity is not used as the responsible contact (confirmed, trace action 95).
When creating for a delegated org, the Importer is auto-populated as the delegated org itself (IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1) (confirmed, trace action 95 / dashboard action 90).
A notification created on behalf of a trade partner is tagged 'Trade Partner' (badge under CHED status) and lists the delegated org as Consignee on the agent's dashboard (confirmed, trace action 90).

Trade-partner relationships are managed via a dedicated 'Manage trade partners' area (/protected/notifications/trade-partners/filter) linked from the signed-in header (confirmed, header nav all snapshots).

This trace does not by itself prove the reciprocal visibility rule (whether a Plant Org 1 member can see the agent-created notification); that requires the paired visibility trace. Recorded as a gap for the cross-actor visibility assertion.
notes: kind=creation but the value is the agent-access surface: two-step org selector (consignment-for → consignment-organisation), Trade Partner dashboard badge, and delegated-org auto-population of responsible person + importer. Standard CHED-PP body pages (country of origin=France, commodity 06011010, manual entry, internal market purpose, packages, transport by rail/road with BCP + inspection premises, CHED reference document REF-124, consignor/exporter 'Linus George Ltd', declaration) were walked but are already covered by the main run — only the delegated surfaces above are new. All org names/addresses are IPAFFS test fixtures.
gaps: Reciprocal visibility (Plant Org 1 member seeing an agent-created notification vs an agent's own-org notification) is asserted by the paired auth/DoA visibility trace, not this one.

Exact business rule for WHICH member of the delegated org becomes the 'Responsible person' (here Isabel Irwin) is not shown — inferred to be a designated org contact; a question for a human / the DoA spec.
